production_contract: Application code is baked into the image and mounted at /app; writable runtime data belongs under /data.
Production runtime expects an external PostgreSQL instance; docker-compose.yml does not provision Postgres.
Production runtime expects an external OpenClaw Gateway; Aries delegates execution across that boundary.
Production Compose defaults the gateway Lobster cwd to aries-app/lobster and should not rely on source bind mounts for normal runtime.
local_contract: Host local development uses NODE_ENV=development, PostgreSQL 16, DATA_ROOT=/tmp/aries-data, and localhost origins for APP_BASE_URL/NEXTAUTH_URL/AUTH_URL.
Host local workflow execution keeps a split between gateway cwd and local checkout paths; gateway-client normalizes absolute lobster paths to aries-app/lobster for gateway calls.
Docker local development publishes port 3000, bind-mounts the repo, and defaults OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL to http://host.docker.internal:18789.
The dev script already includes --turbopack via package.json, so npm run dev is now the canonical local dev command in both host and Docker-dev contexts.
local_host_contract: Use local PostgreSQL 16 and run npm run db:init before first run.
Export CODE_ROOT to the checkout root, DATA_ROOT=/tmp/aries-data, and localhost auth/base URLs.
Use npm run dev for local Next.js + Turbopack.
local_docker_contract: Use docker compose with docker-compose.yml plus docker-compose.local.yml.
Repo bind mounts are dev-only and the writable runtime mount remains /data.
Gateway access defaults to host.docker.internal for local Docker runs.
The aries-app-dev profile now inherits Turbopack through npm run dev.

real_workflow_targets.notes: Current executable contract is one marketing-pipeline.lobster run followed by resume tokens for strategy, production, launch review, and paused publish approvals.
Approvals resume the same pipeline rather than jumping to separate stage-specific workflow files.

validation_snapshot.failing_test: tests/marketing-local-dev-regression.test.ts :: runOpenClawLobsterWorkflow falls back to the local Lobster CLI when the gateway is unavailable
validation_snapshot.failing_reason: Local fallback hit brand_kit_fetch_failed:fetch failed during marketing-pipeline execution.

drifts.summary: backend/openclaw/workflow-catalog.ts still advertises stage-2/3/4 nested workflow files, but the current executable marketing contract is marketing-pipeline.lobster plus resume tokens. Those nested workflow files are not present in lobster/.
Several UI-facing operational routes are still wired to parity stub workflows and return workflow_missing_for_route / not_implemented behavior instead of production workflows.

The checked-in manifests and runtime docs describe a narrower route surface than the app and tests actually expose, including canonical dashboard review routes, redirect aliases, and the public contact page/API placeholder behavior.
README-runtime.md still describes Aries as a Next.js 15 runtime while package.json pins next 16.1.7 and README.md already calls out the newer version.
README.md still warns that aries-app-dev uses npm run dev without --turbopack, but package.json now includes --turbopack in the dev script, so that caveat is stale.
The targeted local-dev regression suite currently fails when gateway-less marketing-pipeline fallback resumes locally; the failure observed was brand_kit_fetch_failed:fetch failed.

governance_note: Execution labels like aries-prod are subordinate specialist/task labels only. Persistent owners remain Jarvis, Forge, Signal, or Ledger.
notes_for_prod: Freeze the production contract around the real marketing pipeline: marketing-pipeline.lobster + resume-token approvals.
Decide which currently stubbed UI-facing routes must become real before ship versus which should be removed or explicitly demoted from the supported contract.
Reconcile route docs/manifests with the actual tested route surface so production handoff artifacts stop drifting from executable truth.
Keep production assumptions aligned with the repo: code baked into image, /data writable, external Postgres, external OpenClaw Gateway.
notes_for_local: Derive local host and Docker settings only after the production contract is frozen.
Keep gateway cwd and local checkout paths split; gateway-client normalization already supports host absolute paths.
Treat npm run dev as the canonical Turbopack dev entrypoint now that package.json includes --turbopack.
Fix the local Lobster fallback regression before declaring local parity complete.
